Container.Name: cloudflare-ddns-plus
Container.Overview:
Self-hosted dynamic DNS updater for Cloudflare, with a web UI instead of a JSON config file.
It checks your public IPv4/IPv6 on a schedule and creates or updates the matching Cloudflare A / AAAA records, so a home server on a dynamic IP stays reachable through your domain. Zones, subdomains, proxying, TTL, IP-detection provider and schedule are all configured through the UI.
Also includes: per-subdomain A/AAAA control, Cloudflare WAF IP-list syncing, notifications (Discord / Slack / webhook / ntfy) with per-channel event selection, heartbeat monitoring (Healthchecks.io, Uptime Kuma, Better Stack, custom URL), config backup and restore, an activity log, light/dark/paper themes, and an optional tab for other DDNS providers (DuckDNS, FreeDNS, DynDNS2, or any custom update URL).
First run: leave ADMIN_PASSWORD blank and a random password is printed once in the container log, or set one here up front. Everything else is configured in the web UI.
Not affiliated with, or endorsed by, Cloudflare, Inc.

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Stores config.json (including your Cloudflare API tokens) and the session secret. Back this up.

Config.Name: Admin username
Config.Description: Login name for the web UI.

Config.Name: Admin password
Config.Description: Login password for the web UI. Leave blank to have a random one generated and printed once in the container log.

Config.Name: Session secret
Config.Description: Secret used to sign the login cookie. Leave blank and one is generated and saved into the data folder.

Config.Name: Enable other DDNS providers
Config.Description: Set to true to show the optional DDNS tab for DuckDNS, FreeDNS, DynDNS2 and custom update URLs. Off by default; the Cloudflare side is unaffected either way.

Config.Name: Disable update check
Config.Description: Set to true to stop the footer checking GitHub for a newer release (for air-gapped installs).

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Timezone for activity-log timestamps, e.g. Europe/London.
